articles.title: Gemini gets notebooks to help you organize projects
articles.summary: Google's Gemini now features 'notebooks' to organize projects in one place, allowing users to consolidate files, conversations, and custom instructions for AI context. The feature mirrors ChatGPT's Projects feature launched in 2024.

articles.title: OpenAI made economic proposals — here’s what DC thinks of them
articles.summary: OpenAI presented economic proposals to Washington DC officials. The article discusses political reactions to OpenAI's policy recommendations in the nation's capital.

articles.title: Meta is reentering the AI race with a new model called Muse Spark
articles.summary: Meta launched Muse Spark, its new AI model powering Meta AI apps and websites. The model will expand to WhatsApp, Instagram, Facebook, Messenger, and smart glasses.

articles.title: Poke makes using AI agents as easy as sending a text
articles.summary: Poke simplifies AI agent access by allowing users to control tasks and automations through text messages, eliminating complex setup requirements and technical barriers for everyday users.

articles.title: AWS boss explains why investing billions in both Anthropic and OpenAI is an OK conflict
articles.summary: AWS leader justifies investing billions in competing AI companies Anthropic and OpenAI, citing the company's established culture of managing competitive relationships with partners.

articles.title: Tubi is the first streamer to launch a native app within ChatGPT
articles.summary: Tubi becomes the first streaming service to launch a native app within ChatGPT, allowing users to access content directly through the AI chatbot.

articles.title: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
articles.summary: Railway secured $100 million Series B funding to build AI-native cloud infrastructure, challenging AWS with a platform that has attracted two million developers by addressing limitations of legacy cloud providers.

articles.title: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
articles.summary: Claude Code's $20-$200 monthly pricing sparks developer backlash, while Goose offers free, open-source alternative with identical functionality running locally without subscription fees or rate limits.

articles.title: Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
articles.summary: Listen Labs raised $69M Series B funding after a viral billboard campaign featuring AI tokens that led to a coding challenge, attracting thousands of engineers and helping the startup scale hiring.

articles.title: Iran-linked hackers disrupt operations at US critical infrastructure sites
articles.summary: Iranian government-linked hackers are disrupting US critical infrastructure by targeting programmable logic controllers in factories, refineries, and water treatment centers. Multiple US agencies warn of ongoing operational disruptions since March 2026.

articles.title: Thousands of consumer routers hacked by Russia's military
articles.summary: Russia's military-linked APT28 hacked 18,000-40,000 consumer routers globally, redirecting users to credential-harvesting sites for espionage operations targeting governments and organizations.

articles.title: OpenClaw gives users yet another reason to be freaked out about security
articles.summary: OpenClaw, a popular AI agentic tool with 347,000 GitHub stars, poses security risks by requiring broad system access to perform tasks. A recently patched vulnerability highlights dangers of granting such extensive permissions.

articles.title: Mustafa Suleyman: AI development won’t hit a wall anytime soon—here’s why
articles.summary: Mustafa Suleyman argues AI development will continue accelerating due to exponential growth trends rather than linear progression. Training data for frontier AI models has increased trillionfold since 2010, defying human intuition.

articles.title: The Download: water threats in Iran and AI’s impact on what entrepreneurs make
articles.summary: Desalination plants in Iran face vulnerability amid escalating conflict, with threats to water supply affecting agriculture, industry, and drinking water across the Middle East region.

articles.title: Desalination plants in the Middle East are increasingly vulnerable
articles.summary: Middle East desalination plants face escalating vulnerability amid regional conflict. Iran accused the US of attacking a facility, disrupting water supply to villages, highlighting critical infrastructure risks.

articles.title: ALTK‑Evolve: On‑the‑Job Learning for AI Agents
articles.summary: ALTK-Evolve enables AI agents to learn and improve while performing tasks on the job, adapting in real-time without retraining.

articles.title: Safetensors is Joining the PyTorch Foundation
articles.summary: Safetensors, a file format for safely storing and sharing machine learning models, is joining the PyTorch Foundation. This integration aims to standardize model serialization across the ML community.

articles.title: Welcome Gemma 4: Frontier multimodal intelligence on device
articles.summary: Google introduces Gemma 4, a frontier multimodal AI model designed to run efficiently on-device. The advancement enables powerful AI capabilities for vision, text, and audio processing locally.

articles.title: A conversation with Kevin Scott: What’s next in AI
articles.summary: Kevin Scott discusses the future trajectory of AI technology and its potential developments. The conversation explores upcoming innovations and directions in the AI field.

articles.title: From Hot Wheels to handling content: How brands are using Microsoft AI to be more productive and imaginative
articles.summary: Major brands like Hot Wheels leverage Microsoft AI tools to enhance productivity and creative capabilities, enabling more innovative content creation and business operations.

articles.title: Microsoft open sources its ‘farm of the future’ toolkit
articles.summary: Microsoft released its 'farm of the future' toolkit as open source, enabling broader adoption of AI-driven agricultural technologies for sustainable farming practices.

articles.title: New ways to balance cost and reliability in the Gemini API
articles.summary: Google introduces two new inference tiers for Gemini API—Flex and Priority—to balance cost and latency for users.

articles.title: Create, edit and share videos at no cost in Google Vids
articles.summary: Google Vids now offers free AI-powered video creation and editing using Lyria 3 and Veo 3.1, enabling high-quality video generation at no cost.

articles.title: We’re creating a new satellite imagery map to help protect Brazil’s forests.
articles.summary: Google partnered with the Brazilian government to create a satellite imagery map designed to protect Brazil's forests. The initiative aims to monitor and help preserve the country's forest resources.

articles.title: Gemma 4: Byte for byte, the most capable open models
articles.summary: Google released Gemma 4, their most capable open-source models yet, designed for advanced reasoning and agentic workflows.

articles.title: Gemini 3.1 Flash Live: Making audio AI more natural and reliable
articles.summary: Google's Gemini 3.1 Flash Live voice model enhances audio AI with improved precision and lower latency for more natural, fluid voice interactions.

articles.title: Protecting people from harmful manipulation
articles.summary: Google DeepMind researches how AI can harmfully manipulate people in finance and health sectors, implementing new safety measures to prevent misuse.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #238 - GPT 5.4 mini, OpenAI Pivot, Mamba 3, Attention Residuals
articles.summary: OpenAI released GPT-5.4 mini and nano models with 400k-token context windows and improved token efficiency. Mistral open-sourced the Small 4 model family while industry discussions focused on attention residuals and Mamba 3 developments.

articles.title: Last Week in AI #339 - DLSS 5, OpenAI Superapp, MiniMax M2.7
articles.summary: Nvidia unveiled DLSS 5, an AI-powered graphics technology that uses generative AI to enhance photorealism in real-time video games up to 4K resolution by analyzing scene details and lighting conditions.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #237 - Nemotron 3 Super, xAI reborn, Anthropic Lawsuit, Research!
articles.summary: Episode 237 discusses major AI developments including Perplexity's local Mac-based AI agent, Anthropic's GitHub PR pricing, and new interactive features from ChatGPT and Anthropic for math, science, and data visualization.

articles.title: AI’s software development success and central management needs
articles.summary: AI has entered early production phases for many enterprises, but adoption is outpacing governance. Companies must establish controls and integrate AI safely into existing platforms to manage risks.

articles.title: Microsoft open-source toolkit secures AI agents at runtime
articles.summary: Microsoft released an open-source toolkit for securing AI agents at runtime, enforcing governance on autonomous language models that execute code faster than traditional policy controls can manage.

articles.title: Asylon and Thrive Logic bring physical AI to enterprise perimeter security
articles.summary: Asylon and Thrive Logic partner to integrate physical AI robots with intelligent analytics for enterprise perimeter security, enabling autonomous patrols and automated incident response.

articles.title: Which Agent Causes Task Failures and When?Researchers from PSU and Duke explores automated failure attribution of LLM Multi-Agent Systems
articles.summary: Researchers from PSU and Duke developed methods to automatically identify which agent causes task failures in LLM multi-agent systems and when failures occur.

articles.title: ByteDance Introduces Astra: A Dual-Model Architecture for Autonomous Robot Navigation
articles.summary: ByteDance introduced Astra, a dual-model architecture for autonomous robot navigation that addresses localization, self-positioning, and path planning challenges in complex indoor environments.

articles.title: MIT Researchers Unveil “SEAL”: A New Step Towards Self-Improving AI
articles.summary: MIT researchers introduced SEAL, a framework enabling large language models to self-improve by generating training data through self-editing and updating their own weights using reinforcement learning.

articles.title: Why harness engineering is becoming the new AI moat
articles.summary: As large language models become commoditized, sophisticated engineering systems built around them emerge as the true competitive advantage, exemplified by Anthropic's Claude Code leak.

articles.title: How GhostClaw malware targets the OpenClaw AI agent boom
articles.summary: GhostClaw malware exploits macOS binaries to steal credentials from developers running local AI agents on Mac Minis, targeting the growing OpenClaw AI agent ecosystem.

articles.title: Why Meta’s V-JEPA 2.1 model is a massive step forward for real-world AI
articles.summary: Meta's V-JEPA 2.1 advances video self-supervised learning by balancing motion tracking with spatial detail, overcoming historical limitations in AI models.

articles.title: Sigmoid vs ReLU Activation Functions: The Inference Cost of Losing Geometric Context
articles.summary: Sigmoid activation compresses inputs into narrow ranges, losing geometric context needed for deep learning. ReLU preserves magnitude information, enabling deeper networks to remain expressive without excessive computation.

articles.title: A Coding Guide to Build Advanced Document Intelligence Pipelines with Google LangExtract, OpenAI Models, Structured Extraction, and Interactive Visualization
articles.summary: Tutorial on using Google's LangExtract library with OpenAI models to transform unstructured documents into structured, machine-readable data. Demonstrates entity extraction, visualization, and creation of reusable pipelines for contracts, notes, and logs.

articles.title: Google AI Research Introduces PaperOrchestra: A Multi-Agent Framework for Automated AI Research Paper Writing
articles.summary: Google AI introduces PaperOrchestra, a multi-agent system that automatically converts unstructured research materials into submission-ready LaTeX manuscripts, including literature reviews, figures, and verified citations.

articles.title: The best Hisense TVs of 2026: Expert tested and reviewed
articles.summary: Hisense has established itself as a trusted mid-range TV manufacturer and now offers premium-grade screens for high-end home theaters.

articles.title: Why I stopped using 'Modern Standby' on my Windows laptop to save battery overnight
articles.summary: Modern Standby on Windows laptops drains battery overnight despite appearing to be in sleep mode. Traditional sleep modes may better preserve battery life.

articles.title: KDE Linux is the purest form of Plasma I've tested - but the install isn't for the meek
articles.summary: KDE Linux provides the purest version of KDE Plasma without OS-specific customizations, though installation requires technical expertise.

articles.title: A Hands-On Guide to Testing Agents with RAGAs and G-Eval
articles.summary: Guide on testing AI agents using RAGAs and G-Eval frameworks. Covers hands-on methods for evaluating agent performance and reliability.

articles.title: Handling Race Conditions in Multi-Agent Orchestration
articles.summary: Race conditions occur when multiple agents simultaneously write to the same resource, causing corrupted or nonsensical outputs. The article addresses preventing conflicts in multi-agent orchestration systems.

articles.title: Top 5 Reranking Models to Improve RAG Results
articles.summary: Article discusses five reranking models that enhance retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) systems by improving result quality and relevance in information retrieval tasks.

articles.title: Why AI Is Training on Its Own Garbage (and How to Fix It)
articles.summary: AI models are increasingly training on their own generated content, degrading data quality. The article discusses this circular problem and potential solutions to improve training data sources.

articles.title: Detecting Translation Hallucinations with Attention Misalignment
articles.summary: Researchers present a cost-effective method for detecting hallucinations in neural machine translation by analyzing attention misalignment to estimate token-level uncertainty.

articles.title: How to Use Claude Code to Build a Minimum Viable Product
articles.summary: Learn how to use Claude Code, an AI coding agent, to efficiently build minimum viable products and present product ideas effectively.

articles.title: National Robotics Week — Latest Physical AI Research, Breakthroughs and Resources
articles.summary: During National Robotics Week, NVIDIA highlights breakthroughs in physical AI and robotics transforming industries. Advancements in robot learning, simulation, and foundation models enable faster deployment from virtual training to real-world applications.

articles.title: From RTX to Spark: NVIDIA Accelerates Gemma 4 for Local Agentic AI
articles.summary: Google and NVIDIA optimized Gemma 4, a small efficient model family for local on-device AI execution across diverse systems from PCs to data centers, enabling real-time contextual intelligence without cloud dependency.

articles.title: Press Start on April: GeForce NOW Brings 10 Games to the Cloud
articles.summary: GeForce NOW launches 10 new games in April, including Capcom's PRAGMATA and Arknights: Endfield. The cloud gaming service continues expanding its library with fresh titles for streaming.

articles.title: Customize Amazon Nova models with Amazon Bedrock fine-tuning
articles.summary: Amazon Bedrock now enables customization of Amazon Nova models through fine-tuning techniques, allowing businesses to adapt AI models for specific industry needs and proprietary knowledge beyond prompt engineering.

articles.title: Human-in-the-loop constructs for agentic workflows in healthcare and life sciences
articles.summary: AI agents in healthcare require human-in-the-loop oversight to ensure regulatory compliance and patient safety. AWS provides practical approaches for implementing human oversight at critical decision points in clinical workflows.

articles.title: Building intelligent audio search with Amazon Nova Embeddings: A deep dive into semantic audio understanding
articles.summary: Amazon Nova Embeddings enable intelligent audio search by converting audio into numerical vectors that capture acoustic features like tone, emotion, and music, transcending traditional text-based methods.

articles.title: Integrate Physical AI Capabilities into Existing Apps with NVIDIA Omniverse Libraries
articles.summary: NVIDIA Omniverse Libraries enable developers to integrate Physical AI capabilities into existing applications, allowing teams to design and validate robots in simulated environments before deployment.

articles.title: Running AI Workloads on Rack-Scale Supercomputers: From Hardware to Topology-Aware Scheduling
articles.summary: NVIDIA's GB200 and GB300 NVL72 supercomputers use Blackwell architecture with 18 compute trays and high-bandwidth networking for optimized AI workload scheduling and performance.

articles.title: Accelerating Vision AI Pipelines with Batch Mode VC-6 and NVIDIA Nsight
articles.summary: NVIDIA enhances vision AI pipeline performance using batch mode VC-6 and Nsight tools to optimize decode, preprocessing, and GPU scheduling stages alongside model throughput improvements.

articles.title: How generational differences affect consumer attitudes towards ads
articles.summary: A research study with CrowdDNA examines how generational differences influence consumer attitudes toward ads across various social media platforms.

articles.title: Every tree counts
articles.summary: Meta commits to net zero emissions by 2030, developing technology to reduce its carbon footprint and making these solutions publicly available.

articles.title: How a non-traditional background led to cutting-edge XR tech
articles.summary: An individual with an unconventional background developed innovative extended reality technology, demonstrating how diverse experiences can drive breakthroughs in emerging tech fields.

articles.title: Identifying Interactions at Scale for LLMs
articles.summary: Researchers present methods for identifying feature interactions in Large Language Models at scale to improve interpretability and transparency. This work aims to make LLM decision-making processes more understandable for safer and more trustworthy AI systems.

articles.title: Information-Driven Design of Imaging Systems
articles.summary: Research on information-driven design of imaging systems that optimizes optical encoders to map objects to images while handling noise. Uses information estimators to quantify measurement quality for distinguishing objects.

articles.title: RL without TD learning
articles.summary: A new RL algorithm using divide-and-conquer paradigm replaces temporal difference learning, addressing TD's scalability issues and performing better on long-horizon tasks.
